description: The HSTU model introduces a SiLU-gated attention mechanism for sequential recommendation, replacing softmax attention with a SiLU activation on attention scores. This allows masking padded positions with 0.0 instead of -inf, which prevents the NaN problem that can occur when an entire row is masked in softmax attention. The model also normalizes output embeddings using L2 normalization and applies temperature scaling to logits, enabling stable training with sampled softmax. The approach is evaluated on the Amazon Beauty dataset using leave-one-out evaluation, demonstrating its effectiveness for sequential recommendation tasks.
motivation: The motivation is to improve sequential recommendation by avoiding the NaN issue in softmax attention when rows are fully masked, and to stabilize training with L2 normalization and temperature scaling.
implementation_notes: The HSTU model applies SiLU-gated attention (a non-softmax attention mechanism) to sequential recommendation. Unlike standard SASRec which uses softmax attention, HSTU uses SiLU activation on attention scores and masks with 0.0 instead of -inf, avoiding the fully-masked-row NaN problem. Output embeddings are L2-normalized and logits are temperature-scaled for stable sampled softmax training. The model is applied to the Amazon Beauty dataset with leave-one-out evaluation.
pseudocode: Not provided in the content, but the core ideas are described.
